matches.match_rationale: The core coordination problem is inconsistent brand messaging — each campus writes its own copy with different tone, terminology, and timing. Text generation can produce a consistent library of campaign copy variants (email sequences, social posts, flyers) from a single approved brand brief, then allow site-level personalization within guardrails. This directly attacks the duplication-of-effort and conflicting-messaging pain mechanisms.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A centralized campaign content engine: corporate marketing inputs a brief and brand guidelines, AI generates approved copy variants per campaign; site coordinators pick from the library rather than writing from scratch, eliminating conflicting independent efforts.

matches.match_rationale: The pain explicitly includes lack of a central approval process and calendar — this is a workflow coordination problem, not just a content problem. A task automation agent can orchestrate the multi-step campaign approval workflow: intake request from site, route to corporate for brand review, publish to shared calendar, notify site coordinators of approved windows, and flag conflicts between simultaneous campus campaigns. This addresses the silo and approval-process gap directly.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-driven campaign intake and approval pipeline: site submits campaign request, agent checks calendar for conflicts across all 10-15 campuses, routes for brand compliance review, and schedules publication — replacing ad-hoc email coordination with an auditable workflow.

matches.match_rationale: Coordinating campaign timing across 10-15 campuses involves simultaneous hard constraints (no two campuses running conflicting promotions in the same market, budget windows, enrollment deadlines) and soft constraints (preferred channel mix, brand calendar blackout dates). Constraint satisfaction finds valid campaign schedules that respect all these requirements simultaneously — something a manual shared spreadsheet cannot reliably do at scale.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A campaign calendar optimizer: input all site requests, budget limits, market overlap rules, and brand calendar; system outputs a conflict-free multi-site campaign schedule with recommended timing adjustments, replacing manual coordination overhead.

matches.match_rationale: With 10-15 campuses independently producing outreach materials, corporate marketing needs a scalable way to audit whether submitted content aligns with brand standards before it goes out. Text classification can automatically flag off-brand messaging, unapproved claims, or tone violations in site-submitted copy — replacing manual review that is too slow to catch conflicts before publication.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated brand compliance checker: site-submitted campaign copy is scored against brand guidelines (tone, key messages, prohibited phrases) before approval, with flagged items routed for human review and clean submissions fast-tracked.
